response:
KEEP THE AREA CLEAN: BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY. RINSE MOUTH WITH WATER TO REMOVE FOOD. AVOID HARD FOODS TO PREVENT DAMAGE.  
*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
BOX TOOTH TO PROTECT IT FROM FURTHER HARM. USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS TO COVER THE VOID. FOLLOW INSTRUCTIONS TO AVOID MORE PAIN.  
* * *  
FLOSS CAREFULLY AND AVOID STICKY FOODS. STAY AWAY FROM HOT OR COLD BEVERAGES. CHOOSE TO CHOOSE THE OPPOSITE SIDE TO CHOOSE TO CHOOSE.  
*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
SALTWATER RINSING REDUCES INFLAMMATION AND BACTERIA. MIX SALT WITH WARM WATER TO MAKE A SOLUTION. RINSE GENTLY TO CLEANSE THE MOUTH.  
* * *  
PAIN MANAGEMENT IS IMPORTANT TO REDUCE DISCOMFORT. USE IBUPROFEN OR ACETAMINOPHEN AS DIRECTED. AVOID ASPIRIN TO PREVENT BLEEDING.  
*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
TRADITION OF GOOD HYGIENE HELPS PREVENT DISEASE. TWO MAIN STEPS ARE CLEANING AND RINSING. CAR IS NOT A PLACE TO SEEK DENTAL CARE.  
* * *  
MONITOR FOR ANY CHANGES IN THE TOOTH. LOOK FOR SWELLING OR BAD TASTE. SEEK DENTAL CARE BEFORE IT GETS WORSE.  
*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
DENTAL EMERGENCIES NEED IMMEDIATE ATTENTION. SEVERE PAIN OR FEVER IS A WARNING SIGN. DON’T DELAY TREATMENT TO AVOID COMPLICATIONS.
